canonical_question: When should you use SAP BTP Integration Suite vs a standalone iPaaS like MuleSoft or Boomi?
aliases: SAP Integration Suite vs MuleSoft vs Boomi comparison
Should I use SAP CPI or a third-party iPaaS
SAP BTP Integration Suite vs standalone iPaaS decision framework
Clean core integration strategy SAP vs MuleSoft

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: 2024 — SAP BTPEA replaced CPEA for new contracts; Boomi acquired by TPG Capital

constraints: SAP Integration Suite is message-based: each outgoing message up to 250 KB = 1 message; larger payloads counted in 250 KB slices
MuleSoft pricing is vCore-based: minimum 0.2 vCores for design, 1 vCore per CloudHub worker for runtime
Boomi pricing is per-connector-class and deployment model — not directly comparable to message-based models
SAP pre-built content packages (2,500+) are free only if unmodified — any customization triggers full message counting
MuleSoft SAP connector requires separate SAP JCo/IDoc libraries and license
Non-SAP-to-non-SAP routing through SAP IS is technically possible but economically wasteful
Clean core mandate: SAP RISE contracts increasingly require SAP BTP Integration Suite for certified integrations

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs detailed SAP Integration Suite capabilities, adapters, and message counting rules
User needs SAP S/4HANA API-level details (OData, RFC, IDoc endpoints)
User needs a general iPaaS comparison without SAP context

often_confused_with.label: SAP Integration Suite Capabilities (this card is about WHEN to use it, not HOW)

inputs_needed.question: What is your SAP footprint?
options: SAP core ERP + non-SAP satellites (Salesforce, Workday, etc.)
Minimal SAP (one SAP system among many non-SAP)

inputs_needed.question: Are you on or migrating to SAP RISE / Clean Core?
options: Yes — RISE with SAP / S/4HANA Cloud
ECC still, no RISE plans yet

inputs_needed.question: What percentage of integrations involve SAP?
